instruction: Go through the door and turn left. Go to the left of the stairs. Stop in the doorway to the left of the white double doors.

system_prompt:
You are controlling a robot in a real indoor environment (a photorealistic 3D scan of a building). You interact only through these tools:

- observe(): look through the robot's forward-facing camera (RGB image plus a clearance readout: meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view; 10.0 = open).
- step(actions): execute movement actions in order. 0 = STOP (permanently ends the episode — declares you have reached the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.
- look_around(): one call returning four labeled views (ahead / right / behind / left); rotates 360 degrees and restores your heading (costs 24 turn steps).

Your task is to follow this navigation instruction to its endpoint:

"Go through the door and turn left. Go to the left of the stairs. Stop in the doorway to the left of the white double doors. "

Rules:
- Alternate observing and stepping: look, decide where the instruction wants you to go next, move, look again.
- You have a budget of 500 movement actions; each step() result reports roughly how many remain.
- You succeed only if you issue action 0 (STOP) while within 3 meters of the instruction's endpoint. STOP is permanent — issue it only when you believe you are at the goal.
- Turning in place (e.g. step([2,2,2,2,2,2])) is a cheap way to look around when unsure.
- Work autonomously until you stop; nobody can answer questions.
first_prompt: Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.

tool_schemas.name: observe
tool_schemas.description:
Look through the robot's forward-facing camera.

Returns the current egocentric RGB view plus a clearance readout: distance in meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view (10.0 = open, 10 m or more). If the goal object is centered ahead, clearance "center" is your true distance to it. Pure read — does not advance the simulator or consume step budget.

tool_schemas.name: look_around
tool_schemas.description:
Scan the surroundings in ONE call: rotates a full 360 degrees and
    returns four labeled views — ahead (0°), right (+90°), behind (+180°),
    left (+270°) — then restores the original heading exactly.

    Costs 24 low-level turn steps from the step budget but only one tool
    call. Use this at junctions and decision points instead of turning 15
    degrees at a time; your position and final heading are unchanged.

tool_schemas.name: step
tool_schemas.description:
Execute a sequence of movement actions, in order.

Actions: 0 = STOP (permanently ENDS the episode — issue it only when you believe the robot is within 3 meters of the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.

Executes sequentially and halts early if the episode ends. Returns how many actions ran, total steps taken, remaining budget, and whether the episode is over. The camera view changes after stepping — call observe() to see the result. Note: when plenty of budget remains, your FIRST STOP request is withheld pending a placement check — call step([0]) again to confirm and execute it.

text: Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.
I'll start by observing my surroundings.
I'm in a bathroom. I need to find the door to go through. Let me scan around to get a full picture.

The ahead view shows the most open space with a doorway visible on the left side (there's a door near the toilet area). The left view shows a bathtub and an opening/doorway on the right. The ahead direction seems the way out — there's a door on the left side of the ahead view.

Let me look at the ahead view again. There's a door frame on the left near the toilet. Let me move forward toward the open area.
I moved toward the toilet, which is a dead end. The door out must be elsewhere. Looking at the original ahead view, there was a doorway on the far left. Let me turn left to find the exit door.
Now I can see a doorway ahead-left leading out of the bathroom to a hallway/landing area with railings (stairs area). Let me turn a bit more left to face the door opening.
Now I can see the doorway ahead leading to a landing with a white railing (staircase area). There's a bathtub on my left. The door/opening is ahead. Let me move forward through the doorway.
I'm now at the doorway. Beyond it is a landing area with white railings (stairs). The clearance is tight. Let me continue forward through the doorway.

I'm at the doorway edge now. There's a wall directly ahead (center clearance 0.5). To my right is more open space (1.8m). The instruction says "Go through the door and turn left." I've gone through the door. Now I should turn left. But the left is a wall. Let me look around to understand the layout better - the stairs/railing is to my right.

Now I understand the layout better:
- Behind me is the bathroom (with vanity/sink).
- Right (+90°) shows a white door with handle - this could be part of the "white double doors".
- Ahead is the landing with railing and stairs.

I'm still at the bathroom doorway. The instruction says "Go through the door and turn left. Go to the left of the stairs." I need to get out onto the landing where the stairs are. The stairs/railing area is ahead-right. 

Let me first fully exit the bathroom. The ahead-right is the open landing. Let me turn right slightly and move toward the open landing area.

I'm on the landing now. I can see the railing/staircase ahead. This is the stairs area. The instruction says to go "to the left of the stairs" and stop in the doorway to the left of the white double doors.

Let me look around to locate the stairs and the white double doors and figure out which way is "left of the stairs."

Now I have a clearer picture:
- Ahead: railing / stairwell.
- Right (+90°): a white door (could be the white double doors).
- Behind: bathroom.
- Left (+270°): an open hallway/corridor with more rooms and doorways (clearance 3.1 on the right of that view).

The instruction: "Go through the door and turn left. Go to the left of the stairs. Stop in the doorway to the left of the white double doors."

The white door is to my right. The stairs are ahead. "Left of the stairs" — I need to move so I'm on the left side of the stairs. The left view shows an open corridor with a doorway leading to a room. That corridor is where I should head.

Let me turn left toward the open corridor and explore.

I'm now facing the corridor. There's a wall/column on the left (clearance 0.3-0.4) and the open corridor to the right (3.1). I need to move toward the open area. Let me turn right a bit to face the open corridor properly.

Now I can see clearly. Ahead there's:
- On the right, a white double-door cabinet/wardrobe (the "white double doors").
- On the left, a doorway/opening leading to another room (bedroom visible).

The instruction says "Stop in the doorway to the left of the white double doors." The white double doors are on the right, and there's a doorway to the left of them (the opening leading to the bedroom). 

Let me move forward to approach and position at that doorway to the left of the white double doors.

Now I can clearly see:
- The white double doors are on the right/center (the tall white closet/double doors).
- To their left is a doorway/opening leading into a room (bedroom with bed visible).

I need to stop in the doorway to the left of the white double doors. Let me move forward and slightly left to position myself in that doorway.

I'm now very close to the doorway. The white double doors are on the right, and the doorway to the room (bedroom) is directly ahead-left of me. I'm right at the entrance to this doorway. Let me move forward a bit more to be right in the doorway.

I'm now right in the doorway to the left of the white double doors. I can see into the bedroom ahead (with a sofa/chair and bed). The white double doors are on my right. This is exactly where the instruction says to stop: "Stop in the doorway to the left of the white double doors."

I'm positioned in this doorway. This is the goal. Let me issue STOP.

texts: {"stop_withheld": true, "message": "STOP not executed (first request). You still have 73 tool calls \u2014 verify placement before committing: (1) look_around(); (2) re-read the instruction's FINAL clause word by word; (3) apply the placement rules (several similar spots -> the one farther along your travel direction; 'all the way / to the end' -> keep going until center clearance < 1.5 m; 'door/doorway' -> stand at the opening). Move if a better spot exists, then call step([0]) again to execute it.", "tool_calls_used": 27, "tool_calls_remaining": 73}

text: Let me verify placement. The final clause: "Stop in the doorway to the left of the white double doors." I'm in the doorway leading to the bedroom, with the white double doors to my right. Let me do a look_around to confirm.

Now I have a clear confirmation:
- Right (+90°): The white double doors (tall white closet/double doors) are clearly to my right.
- Ahead & Left: The doorway leading into the bedroom.
- Behind: The stairs landing (railing visible), which I came from — confirming I went to the left of the stairs.

The instruction: "Stop in the doorway to the left of the white double doors." The white double doors are on my right, and I'm standing at the doorway that is to the left of them. I'm right at the opening of the doorway. This matches perfectly.

I'm correctly placed. Let me confirm STOP.
